scope.target: Machine-readable example evidence tiers and provenance-aware classification
constraints: Do not equate provenance with verification
Tier declarations must be machine-readable through a manifest
Every retained executable example needs an appropriate test
Every structure-validated item names its validator
Documentation-only examples need no test but cannot claim executable verification
Unclassified legacy material defaults to historical or illustrative and never defaults upward
Do not create a reference product solely to obtain executable evidence
Preserve deliberate BDDfy and Gherkin modes
non_goals: Consolidate duplicate catalogs and fixtures in this task

steps: Define the five evidence tiers and machine-readable schema
Fail closed on missing validator identity for structure-validated items
Classify Java/EzDDD/Lab material by provenance and active role
Test only retained executable artifacts with real build routes
Use minimal inline analyzer/validator inputs for deterministic rules
validation: Evidence metadata parse and enum validation
No untested executable item labeled verified
No historical provenance treated as current product truth
execution.last_completed_step: Machine-readable evidence schema and manifest were added, legacy defaults were set downward, unsupported verified claims were removed, and stale .versions.json metadata was retired.
execution.next_action: Proceed to BUILD-001; EXAMPLE-002 retains duplicate catalog and fixture consolidation.

results.summary: Established five machine-readable evidence tiers, classified top-level example families, defaulted unclassified legacy material to historical, removed unsupported Verified Templates claims, and retired stale source-sync metadata.
tests_run: Unsupported verified-claim and stale .versions scans returned no matches
results.residual_risk: Independent verification is pending; duplicate catalog and fixture maintenance remains in EXAMPLE-002.
